name: Monitoring and Logging
description: Comprehensive logging and monitoring requirements for OT environments. Covers audit log generation per OT component, centralised log collection, SIEM integration, and the specific OT monitoring considerations (passive-only, no active probing, OT protocol-aware analysis).

requirements.name: OT log sources and collection
requirements.description: Identify all OT log sources and implement centralised log collection. OT log sources include: Windows event logs from HMI/EWS, SCADA application logs, historian change logs, OT network device logs, firewall logs, and OT monitoring platform alerts.

technical.summary: Centralised syslog collection from all OT network devices and Windows-based OT systems. OT monitoring platform events forwarded to SIEM.
actions: Deploy centralised syslog server in IDMZ (not in OT zone — write-only from OT perspective)
Configure syslog forwarding from: OT firewalls, OT switches, HMI workstations, SCADA servers, historian server, engineering workstations
Forward OT monitoring platform alerts to SIEM
Retain logs minimum 90 days locally; 1 year in central SIEM; 7 years for incident-related logs (NACSA s26)
Define minimum log events per source: authentication events, configuration changes, service starts/stops, security software events

governance.summary: Log collection coverage reviewed quarterly. Gaps documented as risk items.
actions: Log infrastructure availability monitored — loss of logging is a security event

evidenceItems: Log source register with collection status

slMapping.sl2: Centralised syslog; 90-day minimum retention; authentication and change events captured
slMapping.sl3: Centralised SIEM with OT platform integration; real-time correlation; 1-year retention
slMapping.sl4: Immutable log storage; forensic-quality log preservation; 7-year retention for incident logs

requirements.name: SIEM integration and OT correlation rules
requirements.description: Integrate OT log sources with the Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) platform and implement OT-specific correlation rules that detect OT attack patterns.

technical.summary: SIEM includes OT-specific detection rules: unusual engineering connections, off-hours PLC programming access, new device appearing on OT network, authentication failures on SCADA, large data transfers from OT to IT.
actions: Onboard all OT log sources to SIEM with appropriate parsers for OT log formats

Implement OT-specific detection rules: new device on OT network, off-hours engineering access, PLC program read/write outside change window, unusual data volumes from historian to IT side, authentication failures on OT systems, SCADA service restarts
Correlate OT events with IT events (e.g., compromise on IT + unusual OT access = potential lateral movement)
Tune detection rules against OT operational patterns to reduce false positives (OT SIEM needs OT expertise to tune effectively)
Define response playbooks for each OT alert type

governance.summary: SIEM OT correlation rules reviewed quarterly by OT security and SOC teams.
actions: Rule updates when new OT threat intelligence received

slMapping.sl2: Basic SIEM with OT log sources; manual review
slMapping.sl3: OT-specific correlation rules; automated alerting; SOC with OT expertise
slMapping.sl4: Dedicated OT threat detection platform; AI/ML behavioural analysis; 24/7 OT SOC
